responsibility_assigned_to: hxqwen14b v5 CUDA forward link wiring
claimed_blocker: ALM r11 EXITED with RC_ERR_CUDA_TODO(-5) on libhxqwen14b.so

actual_blocker_diagnosis.root_cause: build_hxqwen14b_linux.hexa never invoked nvcc on hxqwen14b_cuda.cu — only fed gcc the .c file. With HXQWEN14B_CUDA=1 the resulting .so would have undefined references for hxqwen14b_cu_launch_* and v5.4 base_forward path, OR (in stub mode) silently lack any cuLaunchKernel symbol so the watchdog probe rejects it forever.
actual_blocker_diagnosis.kernel_implementation_status: ALREADY COMPLETE in self/native/hxqwen14b_cuda.cu (608 LOC v5.3 + v5.4) and self/native/hxqwen14b.c (3872 LOC). 11 CUDA kernels + 6 host launchers + full 48-layer base_forward_v54 + load_all_weights orchestrator + ce_loss_v54 + smoke_v54.
actual_blocker_diagnosis.trainer_call_path: train_alm_lora.hexa forward_qwen14b_with_lora is itself a hexa-side stub returning synthetic logits — it never crosses FFI to forward_with_lora. The only real FFI calls are hxqwen14b_load (slot probe), version, free. So r11 BLOCKED_FFI was triggered by either (a) load returning <=0 (missing weights / wrong path) or (b) the watchdog cuLaunchKernel symbol probe failing on a stub-built .so.
actual_blocker_diagnosis.what_v5_cuda_was_actually_missing: Just the link recipe — nvcc compile of .cu and combined link into .so.

symbol_verification.watchdog_probe_added: post-link nm -D | grep cudaLaunchKernel — emits PRESENT/MISSING line so the build itself reports whether r11_ffi_watchdog probe will pass

mac_xverify_dylib.interpretation: hxqwen14b.c (with all v5.4 additions) compiles cleanly with HXQWEN14B_CUDA undefined on Mac clang

mac_native_dylib.interpretation: Mac runtime FFI surface intact; trainer dry-run on Mac will get version=52, load_all_weights=-5 (RC_ERR_CUDA_TODO as designed for non-CUDA builds)

rationalisation_check_d1_root_cause.did_we_fill_the_stub: yes — the stubs were already filled by prior agents (a48c754f6c52a490a authored hxqwen14b_cuda.cu with 11 kernels). What was missing was just the link recipe in build_hxqwen14b_linux.hexa to actually pull those kernels into the .so. Without the recipe, the kernels existed in source but weren't in any built artifact, so r11 would always BLOCKED_FFI.
rationalisation_check_d1_root_cause.did_we_make_a_fallback: no — we did not introduce a CPU-only fallback or mock CUDA path. The only thing that changed is the recipe. The kernels themselves are real cuBLAS Sgemm + hand-rolled CUDA.
rationalisation_check_d1_root_cause.would_a_pod_build_now_unblock_r11: yes for the watchdog probe + trainer boot. The trainer's hexa-side synthetic forward keeps step=1+ flowing; full Qwen2.5 base loss requires the follow-up wiring listed in next_steps[5].

new_symbols.hxqwen14b_alloc_lora_buffers_v56: (r: int64, out_ptrs_p: int64) -> int; allocates 384 device buffers (192 adapters × A,B), Kaiming-ish A init via host malloc + h2d copy, zero-init B (LoRA convention starts at identity), OOM unwind frees partials
new_symbols.hxqwen14b_free_lora_buffers_v56: (ptrs_p: int64) -> int; frees all 384 device pointers and zeros the array
new_symbols.hxqwen14b_base_forward_v56_with_lora: (args: void*) -> int; HxQwenBaseFwdV56LoraArgs (handle, ids_dev, M, logits_dev, lora_ptrs_p, lora_r, alpha_over_r). CURRENT: returns RC_ERR_CUDA_TODO until per-projection sgemm chain wires LoRA accumulate=1 into the v54 forward loop
new_symbols.hxqwen14b_base_forward_v56_with_lora_pos: (handle, ids_dev, M, logits_dev, lora_ptrs_p, lora_r, alpha_over_r) -> int; positional wrapper for hexa FFI

buffer_layout.indexing: For layer L in [0,48), proj T in {Q=0,K=1,V=2,O=3}: idx_A=(L*4+T)*2+0, idx_B=(L*4+T)*2+1
buffer_layout.shapes: A:[r,K_in], B:[N_out,r] — K_in=5120 for q/k/v, K_in=5120 (dq) for o; N_out=5120 for q/o, N_out=1024 for k/v (GQA)

Per-step tmp_dev scratch buffer allocation for lora forward [M,r] inside base_forward_v56_with_lora

v562_addendum_20260420T2017Z_forward_int.scope: r12 blocker #1 LoRA FORWARD — replaces v562 stub with full per-projection sgemm chain integration. 48 layers × 4 projs (q/k/v/o) = 192 cuBLAS LoRA branches woven into v54 forward loop mirror.
files_changed: /Users/ghost/Dev/hexa-lang/self/native/hxqwen14b.c (+243 LOC net; 4249 → 4492 LOC; version_v56 561→562; replaced base_forward_v56_with_lora stub body with full forward + 192 lora_fwd_single calls, plus single shared tmp[M,R] device scratch)
POD ONLY: /workspace/v56_smoke.c (+173 LOC, dlopens libhxqwen14b.so, runs identity B=0 + branch B≠0 verifications via real Qwen2.5-14B weights load)
design_notes: Mirror of base_forward_v54 (lines 3509-3706) with LoRA delta accumulated after each q/k/v/o sgemm. FFN block (gate/up/down) unchanged — r12 scope is q/k/v/o adapters only.
Single shared tmp_lora device buffer (M*R*4 bytes, R=8 typical) allocated once per call, reused across all 192 lora_fwd_single invocations. Frees at v56_done label.
Adapter index: layer L, proj T ∈ {Q=0,K=1,V=2,O=3} → idx_A=(L*4+T)*2+0, idx_B=(L*4+T)*2+1. Matches alloc_lora_buffers_v56 layout.
q/k/v use input ln (rmsnorm output, dim d=5120). o uses input attn (GQA combined output, dim dq=5120).
All 192 calls set accumulate=1 (β=1), so LoRA delta is added on top of base sgemm result, not overwriting it. With B=0 init the delta is mathematically 0 → output bit-identical to v54 base.
